rows.description: Recepta varia
rows.notes: Page title clearly written at top centre, no monetary columns on this line; very legible.

rows.description: 1788 Novr. 14th Mr. William Harriott's Plate money
rows.notes: Date and description are clear. The aligned amount in the £ s d columns reads unambiguously as 10 0 0.

rows.description: Mr. Bawden's do.
rows.notes: Short description 'Mr. Bawden's do.' clearly visible; amount aligned as 10 0 0 without ambiguity.

rows.description: Dec. 6. of the Revd. Wm. Cooke Dr. Hardinge's Benefaction
rows.notes: Date and benefaction wording legible; amount clearly 110 0 0. Minor uncertainty in spelling of 'Hardinge' but amount alignment is unambiguous.

rows.description: Dec. 31 Increment due at Midsr. coln from the Barrs
rows.notes: Amount columns read as 61 (pounds), 5 (shillings), 2 with a small trailing loop after the pence digit interpreted as a halfpenny (0.5). Description is partially faded/abbreviated; chosen transcription reflects visible letters ('Midsr.' and 'Barrs') but is uncertain.

rows.description: 1789 Jan. 31. of Mr. Bowles Esq. 4 yrs Rent for W. Kitcham
rows.notes: Date and 'Mr. Bowles' phrase clear. 'W. Kitcham' is the best reading of the place name; amount columns clearly show 175 0 0.

rows.description: Jan. 24 Geo. King & Mrs. Jane Engs's Plate money
rows.notes: Date and names largely legible though surname 'Engs' could be 'Engs' or 'Ings'; amount 20 0 0 is clear from alignment.

rows.description: Mar. 27 Mr. John Harriott's Plate money
rows.notes: Name and 'Plate money' phrase clear; amount aligned as 10 0 0. Minor ambiguity in punctuation only.

rows.description: June 3. of Mr. Cooke Dr. St. Beaufort 2d Payment
rows.notes: Description handwriting slightly compressed; interpreted as a 2nd payment related to Mr. Cooke/Dr. Somebody (reading 'St. Beaufort' is uncertain). Amount 110 0 0 is clearly aligned.

rows.description: 11. of Mr. Parsons 4 years of ... (table/repair?) which Mr. O'Neill(?)
rows.notes: Description is partially obscured and highly abbreviated; several names/words uncertain ('Parsons' is clear, remainder guessed). Amount reads clearly as 12 0 0.

rows.description: of Mr. Bowles 2d. Payment for W. Kitcham
rows.notes: Second payment line for Mr. Bowles visible; place name matches earlier 'Kitcham' reading. Amount 175 0 0 clearly aligned.

rows.description: 29 of Mr. Denainbray Deans Fees to House
rows.notes: Surname 'Denainbray' is a best-effort reading of cramped handwriting; 'Deans Fees to House' plausible from visible letters. Amount 12 0 0 is distinct.

rows.description: July 1. of Mr. Vivian Rents increm. to Mids. 1789
rows.notes: Pounds and shillings are clear (103 16). Pence read as '5' with a small loop after it interpreted as a halfpenny (0.5). 'Vivian' and 'increm.' are legible but the full phrase is abbreviated.

rows.description: A quarter part of Mr. Donithorne's Fine for... (forfeited?)
rows.notes: Phrase 'A quarter part of Mr. Donithorne's Fine' is readable; the tail word is faded/abbreviated. Amount 26 0 0 is clearly aligned.

rows.description: Mr. Samuels's Rents increm. for year 1788
rows.notes: Surname and date readable though handwriting is cramped. Amount columns read 23 (pounds), 17 (shillings), 3 with a halfpenny loop (interpreted as 0.5).

rows.description: Oct. 30. of Gibbons, Tanner, for old Iron as by Acct
rows.notes: Names 'Gibbons, Tanner' and 'old Iron' phrase clear; amount aligned as 13 14 0 with good legibility.

rows.description: Wm. Robt. Hasting Acct. for Merton Estate from Mid. 1787 & Mid. 1788
rows.notes: Centered account header clearly visible. Some abbreviations ('Wm. Robt.') are standard and legible; no monetary amounts on this header line.

rows.description: money Rent
rows.notes: Label 'money Rent' followed by amount in columns reading 265 0 0. Very clear alignment and digits.

rows.description: Corn Rent
rows.notes: Label 'Corn Rent' clear; amount reads 05 12 0 (5 pounds, 12 shillings, 0 pence) with neat digits.

rows.description: Total of Years Rent
rows.notes: Sum line 'Total of Years Rent' clearly written and amount aligns to 270 12 0; used as alignment reference for the £ s d columns.

rows.description: Deductions allowed
rows.notes: Section header for subsequent deduction lines; no amounts on this header line.

rows.description: Land Tax
rows.notes: Line reads 'Land Tax' with amount clearly 34 7 0; digits and alignment are unambiguous.

rows.description: Levett Close
rows.notes: Description 'Levett Close' is a best-effort reading of the script (could be variant spelling). Amount reads as 6 pounds, 10 shillings, 10 pence; handwriting for the descriptor is less clear than the digits.

rows.description: Rep. Vic. House
rows.notes: Abbreviated label 'Rep. Vic. House' (Repairs Vicarage House) corresponds to visible letters; amount 7 6 7 is readable though the abbreviation causes mild uncertainty in description.

rows.description: Dit. of Chancel
rows.notes: Line reads 'Dit. of Chancel' (ditto for chancel); amount columns clearly show 0 8 9 with good legibility.

rows.description: Total of Deductions
rows.notes: Subtotal of deductions is neatly written as 48 12 4; line is used as alignment anchor and digits are clear.

rows.description: Remains clear for the Year's Rent
rows.notes: Phrase 'Remains clear for the Year's Rent' is slightly cramped but readable; corresponding amount reads 221 19 8 with clear digits. Slight ink smudge near words but not digits.

Bottom-most grand total at extreme right reads '1094 12 7' with a small halfpenny loop after the pence digit interpreted as 0.5. The description cell is blank for this numerical total. Slight uncertainty due to faint fractional mark.
